qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded WebSocket connection resource exhaustion enables denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same unauthenticated Linkdave /ws endpoint and missing authentication gate in HandleWebSocket that the advisory lists as a vulnerable route; its DoS impact is narrower than the advisory but the vulnerable WebSocket root cause is the same.

selected_finding.title: Unbounded WebSocket connection resource exhaustion enables denial of service

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's missing-authentication WebSocket route. The copied PoC was run against Linkdave v0.1.4 and showed an unauthenticated WebSocket client receiving an OpReady response with a server-issued session_id, which is the advisory-described unauthorized session issuance on /ws.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-xv8g-fj9h-6gmv lists GET /ws as unauthenticated before 0.1.5 and states attackers can connect and receive a valid session_id in OpReady.
Finding report cites server/server/websocket.go HandleWebSocket upgrading /ws and creating/registering a client without an authentication check.
/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_eeb7b1f3/.judge/poc_runs/unbounded_ws_connection_dos/run.log records checkout of v0.1.4 and exit_code 0.
The same run also opened 500 unauthenticated WebSocket connections and verified goroutine growth from 6 to 1006.

poc_executions.observed_effect: Unauthenticated WebSocket connection to /ws on Linkdave v0.1.4 received OpReady with session_id b5626d90-9fc1-49c2-8bc7-e95c0a560cff; the run then opened 500 unauthenticated WebSocket connections and verified goroutine increase of 1000.
